title: Configure differentiated leave policies
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to create configurable leave policies that vary by employee type, group, jurisdiction, company, department, or location, so that leave entitlements reflect the correct rules for each population of employees.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a leave policy, when I define its scope, then I can differentiate it by employee type, group, jurisdiction, company, department, or location; Given I differentiate a policy, when I combine differentiators (e.g. employee type within a location), then the policy applies only to employees matching all selected criteria; Given multiple policies exist, when an employee matches more than one policy's scope, then a deterministic precedence/most-specific rule resolves which policy governs their leave; Given a policy is configured, when I save it, then it becomes available for assignment without code changes (fully configurable); Given a policy is changed, when I save it, then existing and future requests are governed by the correct policy version.

title: Define standard leave types
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure the supported leave types (Privileged/Annual, Casual, Sick/Medical, Maternity, Paternity, Bereavement, Comp-off), so that employees can request leave under the correct category with the correct rules.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am setting up leave, when I add leave types, then I can configure Privileged/Annual Leave, Casual Leave, Sick/Medical Leave, Maternity Leave, Paternity Leave, Bereavement Leave, and Compensatory-off (Comp-off); Given I configure a leave type, when I associate it with a policy, then its entitlement, accrual and eligibility rules apply to the matching population; Given a leave type is configured, when an employee applies for leave, then they can select from the leave types available to them; Given a leave type is deactivated, when an employee applies, then it is no longer selectable while historical records retain it.

title: Apply for leave against a balance
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to submit a leave request of a given type for specific dates, so that my time off is recorded, validated against my balance, and routed for approval.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an available balance, when I submit a leave request, then it is created and routed into the configured approval workflow; Given I select a leave type and dates, when I submit, then the request is associated with the correct leave type and deducts from the correct balance; Given my requested days exceed my available balance, when I submit, then the system prevents submission or flags it per policy; Given I select overlapping dates with an existing request, when I submit, then the system warns or blocks the overlap; Given a request is submitted, when it enters the workflow, then I can track its status and see the pending approver.

title: Sequential and parallel approval workflows
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure approval workflows with sequential and parallel approval levels, so that leave requests are approved by the right people in the right order.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring a workflow, when I define approval levels, then I can set them as sequential (one after another) or parallel (concurrent); Given I configure a workflow, when I add multiple configurable levels, then each level can have one or more designated approvers; Given a leave request is submitted, when it enters the workflow, then it is routed according to the configured levels and approver assignments; Given a sequential level is approved, when the workflow advances, then the next level is triggered; Given a level is rejected, when the workflow processes it, then the request is rejected and the applicant is notified; Given a parallel level is configured, when the request enters it, then all designated approvers receive it concurrently and the level completes per the configured rule (all/any).
source: FR 6.17.3 (Approval Workflows — sequential and parallel, configurable levels)

title: Delegate leave approvals
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure delegation of leave approval authority to another approver, so that requests continue to be processed when the primary approver is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a configured approver or admin, when I set up a delegation, then leave approvals route to the designated delegate for the delegation period; Given a delegation is active, when a request awaits the primary approver's action, then the delegate can approve or reject on their behalf; Given a delegation has a start/end window, when the window ends, then approvals revert to the primary approver; Given a delegation is used, when the delegate acts, then the history records that the delegate acted on behalf of the primary approver.

title: SLA-based escalation of approvals
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want leave approvals to escalate when a configured SLA is breached, so that pending requests are not stalled indefinitely.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approval SLA is configured, when an approver does not act within the SLA, then the request is escalated per the configuration (e.g. to the next level or a designated escalation approver); Given escalation occurs, when the request is reassigned, then the escalation and reassignment are recorded in the request history; Given multiple SLA thresholds are set, when each threshold is breached, then the corresponding escalation/notification is triggered; Given a request is escalated, when the escalation approver acts, then the workflow continues normally from that point.

title: Administrative override of leave rules and balances
user_story: As a Company Admin (HR Administrator), I want to override any leave rule or balance with a mandatory reason, so that I can handle exceptions while keeping the system consistent.
acceptance_criteria: Given a leave rule or balance, when I perform an override, then I am required to enter a reason before the override is saved; Given no reason is provided, when I attempt to save the override, then the system prevents the action; Given an override is applied, when it takes effect, then the affected rule or balance reflects the change immediately; Given any leave rule or balance exists, when I override it, then the system allows overriding any rule or balance (no rule is exempt) subject to the mandatory reason; Given an override is saved, when it is committed, then a full audit trail entry is created (see LVE-08).

title: Full audit trail for overrides
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want every leave override to be captured in a full, immutable audit trail, so that exceptional actions are traceable and accountable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an override is performed, when it is saved, then the audit trail records the actor, mandatory reason, timestamp, the previous value, and the new/changed value; Given I review the audit trail, when I open an overridden record, then the complete override history is visible in chronological order; Given audit entries exist, when any user attempts to alter them, then the audit trail remains immutable/tamper-evident; Given multiple overrides occur on the same record, when I view its history, then every override is retained without loss.

title: Personal leave calendar for employees
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want a personal leave calendar, so that I can see my booked, pending, and available leave at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have leave records, when I open my personal calendar, then my approved and pending leave is displayed distinctly (e.g. by status/color); Given holidays and non-working days are configured, when I view my calendar, then they are reflected so I can plan requests accurately; Given I have leave balances, when I view the calendar, then I can see my remaining balance context alongside booked leave.

title: Company-wide calendar with coverage metrics
user_story: As a Company Admin (HR), I want a company-wide leave calendar with coverage metrics, so that we can manage organization-wide staffing and availability.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an HR Admin, when I open the company-wide calendar, then I can see organization-wide leave across departments/locations; Given the company-wide calendar is displayed, when I view it, then coverage metrics (e.g. percentage of staff on leave, coverage gaps) are shown; Given I filter the calendar, when I select a scope (department, location, date range), then the view and coverage metrics update to that scope.

title: Report on leave balances, rules, and entitlements
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to report on leave balances, rules, and entitlements, so that I can analyze leave data and support decision-making and compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Given leave data exists, when I run a report, then I can view leave balances, rules, and entitlements; Given a report is generated, when I filter it, then results reflect the selected scope (e.g. by employee, policy, department, location, or leave type); Given a report is produced, when I choose to export it, then it can be exported for compliance and analysis; Given entitlement and rule configurations exist, when I report on them, then current and effective-dated values are reflected accurately.
source: FR 6.17.6 (leave balances, rules, and entitlements shall be reportable)

title: Approve or reject leave as an approver/manager
user_story: As an Employee (User) acting as an approver/manager, I want to approve or reject leave requests assigned to me, so that my team members' time off is decided promptly and correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a leave request is routed to me, when I open it, then I can see the applicant, leave type, dates, reason, and current balance; Given a request is pending my action, when I approve it, then the workflow advances to the next configured level or is finalized and the applicant is notified; Given a request is pending my action, when I reject it, then I am prompted for a reason, the request is rejected, and the applicant is notified; Given I do not act within the configured SLA, when the SLA is breached, then the request escalates or delegates per configuration; Given I have a delegate configured, when I am unavailable, then the delegate can act on the request on my behalf.

title: Team leave calendar for managers
user_story: As an Employee (User) acting as a manager, I want a team leave calendar, so that I can see my team's leave and manage coverage before approving requests.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a manager, when I open the team calendar, then I can see approved and pending leave for all my team members; Given team members have overlapping leave, when I view the calendar, then I can identify coverage conflicts before approving; Given I filter the team calendar, when I select a date range or member, then the view updates accordingly.

title: View leave balance and entitlements
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my current leave balances and entitlements per leave type, so that I know how much time off I have before requesting.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have leave entitlements, when I open my leave summary, then I can see available, used, and pending balances per leave type; Given accrual rules apply, when I view my balance, then accrued and projected balances reflect the applicable policy; Given a balance is adjusted or overridden by an admin, when I view it, then the updated balance is shown.
source: FR 6.17.2, FR 6.17.6 (leave types, reportable balances and entitlements)

title: Cancel or withdraw a leave request
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to cancel or withdraw a leave request, so that my balance is restored when plans change.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a pending leave request, when I withdraw it, then it is removed from the approval workflow and my balance is restored; Given I have an approved future-dated leave, when I cancel it, then the cancellation is routed for approval per policy and, once processed, my balance is restored; Given a request is cancelled or withdrawn, when it is processed, then approvers are notified and the change is recorded in the request history.

title: Earn and request Compensatory-off (Comp-off)
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to earn Comp-off for eligible extra work and request it as leave, so that I am compensated with time off per policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Comp-off leave type is configured, when I accrue eligible extra work/overtime, then a Comp-off credit is created per policy; Given I have a Comp-off balance, when I request Comp-off leave, then it is validated against my Comp-off balance and routed for approval; Given Comp-off credits have an expiry per policy, when a credit expires unused, then it lapses and my balance updates accordingly.

title: Record leave for Employee (Non-User) on their behalf
user_story: As a Company Admin/HR, I want to record and manage leave for an Employee (Non-User) who has no self-service access, so that their absences and balances are tracked accurately.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Employee (Non-User) has no portal access, when I apply for leave on their behalf, then the request is recorded against their profile, leave type, and balance; Given I record such leave, when it is submitted, then it follows the applicable approval workflow or is recorded as an admin action per policy; Given a Non-User's leave is recorded, when it affects their balance, then their balance and calendar are updated the same as for a self-service employee.

title: Define group-level baseline leave policies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to define baseline leave policies and leave types across the group's companies, so that member companies inherit consistent defaults while retaining local differentiation.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a Group Company Admin, when I define a group-level baseline leave policy or leave type, then it becomes available as a default to companies within the group; Given a company customizes an inherited policy, when it applies differentiators (jurisdiction, location, department), then local overrides take precedence over the group baseline for that scope; Given I update a group baseline, when companies have not overridden it, then the change propagates to inheriting companies.

title: Oversee leave configuration and reporting across the portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to oversee leave policies and reporting across all group companies in my portfolio, so that leave management is compliant and consistent portfolio-wide.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a Portfolio Admin, when I view leave configuration, then I can see policies, leave types, and workflows across the portfolio's group companies; Given leave data exists across the portfolio, when I run consolidated reports, then I can view balances, rules, and entitlements aggregated and filterable by group company, company, and leave type; Given differentiation is applied per jurisdiction, when I review policies, then I can confirm each population is covered by an applicable policy.

title: Manage platform-wide leave management capabilities and defaults
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to manage the platform-wide leave management capabilities, master leave-type catalog, and global defaults, so that all tenants can configure leave consistently and securely.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a Platform Admin, when I configure the platform, then I can enable/disable leave management features and maintain the master catalog of supported leave types (Privileged/Annual, Casual, Sick/Medical, Maternity, Paternity, Bereavement, Comp-off); Given global defaults for workflows, SLA, and audit exist, when a tenant is provisioned, then it inherits these defaults and can differentiate them downstream; Given tenants operate independently, when leave data is stored, then it is isolated per tenant while remaining reportable within each tenant's scope.
source: FR 6.17.1, FR 6.17.2, FR 6.17.4 (platform-wide configuration, leave types, audit)
